mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in <45% of triggered months despite unemployment falling.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in >60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its trend.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rates and persistently high unemployment creates a liquidity-driven valuation expansion, as cheap capital offsets weak labor demand and pushes equity prices higher.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of months when both conditions hold, or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the CPI-U exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of months following CPI spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll additions signal robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings, generating positive equity returns during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in <40% of months with strong payroll growth, or signal reverses when CPI exceeds 3% annually.
